summary: The best next follow-up with Minghan Xiao is a brief, value-forward message referencing your conversation about builder communities and operator dashboards—ideally within 48 hours while the context is fresh. This matters because hackathon organizers like Minghan are key connectors in the ecosystem; a timely, specific follow-up (perhaps sharing a relevant resource or asking a thoughtful question about his community-building work) transforms a casual encounter into a lasting professional relationship, positioning you as someone who listens and adds value rather than just another attendee seeking favors.

relatedDrafts.content: This is not just another content tool. It is a relationship operating system that helps you meet people, remember context, follow through, and turn the work into clear output. The demo uses a real London hackathon organiser thread to show that loop end to end.

latestMirror.summaryText: This week centered on energized (1), social (1), stretched (1). Strongest lift: After a day of strong product conversations, I felt energized and wanted to lock in the follow-up quickly. Watch-out: After a run of back-to-back conversations, I felt stretched and needed a recovery block before writing again. Next experiment: protect one follow-up block and one recovery block.
